audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed as Job's remembrance of former days: divine intimacy, household abundance, public honor, rescue of poor/orphan/widow, justice as clothing, and respected counsel.

verses.restored: Job again lifted up his discourse and said:
If only I were as in the months of old, as in the days when the Divine guarded me,
when his lamp shone over my head, and by his light I walked through darkness,
as I was in the days of my autumn/ripeness, when the intimate counsel of the Divine was over my tent,
when Shaddai was still with me, and my children were around me,
when my steps were washed with butter, and the rock poured out streams of oil for me.
When I went out to the gate by the city, when I prepared my seat in the square,
young men saw me and hid themselves, and elders rose and stood.
Princes held back words and laid a hand on their mouth.
The voice of nobles was hidden, and their tongue clung to their palate.
For the ear heard and blessed me, and the eye saw and bore witness for me,
because I rescued the poor who cried out, and the orphan who had no helper.
The blessing of the perishing came upon me, and the heart of the widow I made sing for joy.
I put on righteousness, and it clothed me; my justice was like a robe and turban.
I was eyes to the blind and feet to the lame.
I was a father to the needy, and the case I did not know I searched out.
I broke the jaws of the unjust and made him drop the prey from his teeth.
Then I said, I shall expire with my nest, and like the sand multiply my days.
My root was open to waters, and dew lodged all night on my branch.
My glory was fresh with me, and my bow was renewed in my hand.
They listened to me and waited, and they kept silent for my counsel.
After my word they did not speak again, and my speech dropped on them.
They waited for me as for rain, and opened their mouth as for spring rain.
I smiled on them when they had no confidence, and the light of my face they did not cast down.
I chose their way and sat as head; I dwelled like a king among troops, like one who comforts mourners.
